paragraphs.text: Fruugo.com Ltd welcomes the opportunity to provide feedback on the public consultation for the Digital Fairness Act (DFA). Fruugo.com Ltd is happy to answer any additional questions the Commission may have regarding our submission.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: Fruugo understands the importance of the European consumer protection laws that have been strengthened in recent years. However, the European Union's digital and consumer regulatory landscape has grown increasingly complex, with a relentless roadmap of EU laws to navigate.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: For medium sized businesses such as Fruugo, the safe use of digital services remains a priority, however, legislation must be supported by clear, well documented guidance and educational measures. We believe that industry led best practices should be given greater attention together with improved business feedback. Fruugo supports a smarter, risk-based approach to regulation across Europe going forward.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: Amidst some important themes of transparency, there are also ambiguities, overlap, inconsistencies across Member States and critically, a lack of legislative guidance for some of the laws enacted in recent years.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: Fruugo supports a focus towards balancing consumer protection with much needed economic growth and ensuring a level playing field for all digital stakeholders. Fruugo welcomes a drive towards simplification and harmonisation across the EU and we consider that streamlined governance is essential to this.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: Fruugo does not consider that additional digital legal frameworks will support this aim. More legislative frameworks will instead overcomplicate the environment and distract from the prioritisation of consumer protection. Fruugo considers that the Digital Fairness Act should align with President Von der Leyen's emphasis on simplification.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

evidence_quotes.quote: protecting a consumer's ability to make clear, informed choices.

paragraphs.text: Fruugo considers it essential that businesses can innovate more freely whilst protecting a consumer's ability to make clear, informed choices.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: Fruugo would welcome additional guidance clarifying the boundaries between dark patterns and permissible design practices. It is important to consider that personalisation and behavioural design, when used transparently and fairly, can benefit and enhance consumer experience.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: We consider that educational measures are also critical in this area so that end consumers are better rehearsed in navigating digital products and better informed on the principles of self-determination and the safe use of digital services.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: We welcome the European Commission initiative to conduct an EU-wide inquiry into digital wellbeing. We consider that it is critical that stakeholders across all industries and demographics are included in this inquiry so that appropriate risk assessments can then be made.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

evidence_quotes.quote: User controls and user education must be prioritised

paragraphs.text: User controls and user education must be prioritised when making any future decisions with respect to digital product design and features.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: There is a significant risk across Europe currently of differing interpretations and enforcement measures by national authorities and courts. Companies operating in multiple Member States

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: face high administrative burdens and legal costs. This creates unfair conditions and can be crippling on resources and budgets for small and medium enterprises.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: Fruugo strongly encourages simplification measures in order to mitigate against legal uncertainty.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: Simplification will allow digital businesses like Fruugo to make targeted investments in compliance initiatives whilst preserving innovation, growth and market competition. Ensuring that this balance is achieved in future legislation has never been more critical than it is now to growing both national and European economies.

section_path: Digital Fairness Act- Fruugo.com Ltd response
DFA Consultation Submission

paragraphs.text: The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
